Name: Opel
1Q: What was the name of the Opel brand founder?
tip1: Adam Opel did not develop any cars, as the company stared operate in the auto industry after his death.
2Q: In which German city are Opel's headquarters and development center?
tip2: Opel has a sister company Vauxhall in the UK, Holden in Australia and Buick in the USA.
3Q: What was Opel's first turbocharged car?
tip3: A refined version of the 2.3-liter diesel engine was Opel's first turbocharged engine.
4Q: In association with which brand Opel made Omega the fastest sedan in the 80s?
tip4: Omega Lotus was faster than the competing Mercedes and BMW because they accept the top speed limit deal.
5Q: What is the name of Opel's first all-terrain vehicle?
tip5: Opel Frontera is made on the chassis of Japanese manufacturer Isuzu.
6Q: What model is the successor of the Opel Kadett?
tip6: The last Opel Kadett is the model E, and first Astra continues as model F.
7Q: What is name of the Opel sport team participating in rally, DTM and other motorsport competitions?
tip7: Opel Motorsport team worked with experts from Irmscher and Cosworth to achieve racing success.
8Q: Which engine uses the Insignia A OPC?
tip8: Except for the OPC, the Insignia is also available with improved off-road capabilities as Country Tourer.
9Q: Which group bought Opel Automobile GmbH from General Motors in 2017?
tip9: A couple of years later, the French PSA merged with the Italian-American FCA group.
10Q: What concept was introduced by Opel CEO Michael Lohscheller to announce the electrification of all models by 2024?
tip10: The GT X concept is also a design guideline for future Opel models.
